dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7897 IH: No Tax on Drill Pay Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Barr (for himself, Mr. Miller of Ohio, and Mr. Nunn of Iowa) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to exclude from gross income compensation received in connection with inactive-duty training.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No Tax on Drill Pay Act.

section.header: Exclusion from gross income of inactive-duty training compensation

subsection.text: Section 134(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.text: The term qualified military benefit includes any compensation received in connection with inactive-duty training (as defined in section 101(d)(7) of title 10, United States Code, as in effect at the time of the receipt of such compensation).

subsection.text: Section 134(b)(3)(A) of such Code is amended by striking paragraphs (4) and (5) and inserting paragraphs (4), (5), and (7).
The amendments made by this section shall apply to compensation received after the date of the enactment of this Act.
